narrative: OMX30 enters Monday in a conflicted technical posture. While the index remains +7.9% above its 200-day MA and shows strong 90-day momentum (+9.1%), weak ADX (1.8) with bearish directional bias and MACD falling below signal strip conviction from the bull setup. Global cues are mixed: NASDAQ +0.89% supports tech sentiment, but CAC 40's -0.87% and oil's sharp +2.80% rally create crosscurrents. Rising geopolitical tension (Iran/Hormuz) has spiked VIX to 18.08 (+6.42%), while US yields fell 27bp to 4.378%, easing some refinancing pressure but not enough to offset energy cost headwinds. The index sits 4.8% from its 52-week high and only +0.3% above the 50-MA—near-term support will be critical.
morningBrief: OMX30 faces a cautious open on Monday amid mixed global signals and elevated geopolitical risk. The index is structurally bullish (7.9% above 200-MA, ROC-90 +9.1%) but tactically weakened: ADX 1.8 (near-term directional void), MACD below signal, and RSI 43.88 neutral with downside bias. Oil's +2.80% spike weighs on industrials (28% of index) and consumer stocks; however, stronger USD/SEK (+0.30%) provides modest tailwind for exporters (Volvo, Atlas Copco, Ericsson). VIX jumped 6.42%—watch for risk-off rotation into defensives (AstraZeneca, Essity) and banks benefit from higher rates despite recession concerns. Key levels: support at 3085 (lower Bollinger), resistance at 3180 (50-MA). Expect range-bound trading until clarity on Iran tensions and this week's data (US Jobless Claims Wed 14:30 CET). Reduce conviction by 30% due to weak ADX—this is a trader's market, not a trend day.
drivers: Weak ADX (1.8) undermines directional conviction — reduce all magnitude calls by 30%
MACD below signal with negative histogram (-8.537) signals near-term momentum loss
Oil +2.80% pressures industrials/consumer; USD/SEK +0.30% supports exporters but insufficient offset
VIX spike +6.42% to 18.08 reflects geopolitical risk (Iran/Hormuz tensions); rising yields compress tech/real estate
RSI 43.88 neutral but approaching oversold — mean reversion risk if selling accelerates

sectorImpacts.reason: Oil +2.80% elevates input costs; weak ADX reduces confidence. Strong USD/SEK (+0.30%) provides partial offset but insufficient given energy spike.
US 10Y yield decline (-27bp to 4.378%) slightly compresses NIM expectations, but rising VIX may boost safe-haven appetite for Nordics' stable credits.
NASDAQ +0.89% supports sentiment, but elevated VIX (+6.42%) and falling yields (DCF compression risk) create headwinds. Weak ADX limits upside conviction.
Geopolitical risk and VIX spike trigger defensive rotation; these names outperform in risk-off environments.
Gold -1.27% and copper -0.58% offset by oil +2.80%. Weak ADX removes clarity; watch Boliden for directional breaks.
Oil +2.80% pressures consumer spending; weak EUR/SEK (EUR down vs SEK) does not help H&M sourcing costs. VIX spike adds discretionary headwind.
Falling US yields (-27bp) ease refinancing concerns; however, weak ADX and elevated geopolitical risk create near-term uncertainty.

econCalendarAlert: No major Swedish data releases Monday. US Jobless Claims (exp. 217k, prev. 212k) at 14:30 CET Wednesday — volatility may spike if print misses materially.
topRisk: Geopolitical escalation (Iran/Hormuz tensions) could spike VIX above 20, triggering broad risk-off and crushing equities. Oil supply concerns could drive energy sharply higher, pressuring all Nordic industrials and consumer demand. Weak ADX means low conviction—stop-losses critical.
technicalSummary: Bull market structure intact: +7.9% above 200-MA, ROC-90 +9.1%
Near-term momentum loss: MACD below signal (hist=-8.537), RSI 43.88 neutral-leaning weak
Weak directional bias: ADX 1.8 with bearish DI dominance — reduce all magnitude forecasts by 30%
Bollinger %B=0.40 in lower half — space for move down to support, but also upside recovery potential
52-week range: 81% utilized, -4.8% from high — profit-taking risk on rallies
